affected_groups: Parents and school communities (trust in system transparency)
Rural and church-maintained school providers (risk of being disadvantaged without consultation)
content.decision_question: Political feasibility versus transparency/equity accountability — convenience of inaction weighed against the need for verifiable, honest reporting on what is actually changing the system
content.evidence_boundary: Contested/unknown: no transparent methodology currently exists to decompose observed shrinkage of selective tracks into demographic-driven versus policy-driven components, and it is unknown whether the Commission would accept passive contraction plus reporting as an adequate response to its selectivity finding
content.tension: Demographically-driven contraction is politically convenient (lowest reversal risk, no legislative cost) but leaves EU-level equity criticism of Hungary's highly selective school system unanswered unless paired with transparent reporting distinguishing demographic effects from active policy levers.
content.title: Decision tension A16: S5
value_poles: transparency/equity accountability — convenience of inaction weighed against the need for verifiable, honest reporting on what is actually changing the system
